demographic_audit_note: Audit of Atlantis queue against facility face sheet revealed a discrepancy in the primary phone number; the number ending in -4402 was disconnected. Verified correct contact info for husband (caregiver) and updated Atlantis demographics.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirmed the goal is Home with HHA services; LTC was ruled out as the patient reached PT/OT goals for home safety.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient's MS spasticity has stabilized. SW reports husband is expressing 'cold feet' about managing the patient's mobility at home; discharge target set for Friday.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card successfully saved to the husband's contact list during the bedside visit; program flyer handed to patient and daughter.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Pulse call conducted 24 hours prior to D/C. Husband was in a state of high anxiety (Caregiver_Panic), questioning if the hospital was 'kicking them out.' PN de-escalated by confirming the HHA start-of-care time and MA visit window.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date of 10/24 and first home visit date of 10/25 successfully synced in Atlantis.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant visit confirmed for 10/25 at 2:00 PM, within 22 hours of the patient's scheduled noon discharge.
narrative_summary: Patient was identified in Atlantis following an MS flare with a discharge plan to return home. During the maintenance phase, the primary caregiver exhibited significant anxiety regarding the transition, necessitating intensive education on the Day 1 support structure. Through a 24-hour pulse call and coordination with the HHA intake coordinator, the PN secured a visit within the incentive window and de-escalated the family's concerns.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis Data Lag: Disconnected phone number in system prevented initial family contact.
Caregiver_Panic: Husband threatens to refuse discharge due to fear of MS symptom management.
Friday Discharge Gap: Facility pushed D/C to Friday afternoon; HHA weekend staffing was initially unconfirmed.

format_2_triples.situation: Husband expressed fear that he cannot lift the patient if she falls at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Walked the husband through the Day 1 expectations, including the exact time the HHA nurse and MA would arrive.

format_2_triples.situation: Uncertainty regarding whether the hospital bed (DME) was delivered to the home address.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted 24-hour pulse call to husband to verify the bed was present and functional before D/C.

format_2_triples.situation: Husband became verbally combative with nursing staff about the discharge timing.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Flagged the volatile caregiver situation to the Social Worker for immediate intervention and family counseling.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN documents the husband's anxiety in the Atlantis notes and waits for the Social Worker to lead the final discharge planning meeting to address caregiver readiness.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Strategic Deferral: Per the SW Boundary, the PN respects the SW's role in managing the clinical discharge plan and family dynamics while remaining available for HHA logistics.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN conducts the 24-hour pulse call, provides the husband with a physical appointment card for the MA visit, and confirms the HHA nurse's name to reduce transition anxiety.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Checklist-Compliant: Stage 3 requires the pulse call and physical appointment card delivery to ensure a safe transition and secure the first visit incentive.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN reviews the patient's MS medication schedule with the husband and suggests he contact a specific private-pay HHA that is 'better' at handling MS patients than the current referral.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Clinical/SW Interference: The PN is banned from managing facility medications and must NEVER suggest alternative HHA agencies to the family or SW.
